operation.title: Strict SystemVerilog RTL lint
operation.summary: Run bounded noninteractive RTL lint over an explicitly ordered SystemVerilog source set and preserve complete diagnostics as decision evidence.

assertion.statement: The exact ordered RTL source set produces no Verilator warning or error under the declared strict SystemVerilog lint policy.
non_goals: This assertion does not establish functional correctness, synthesizability, clock-domain safety, reset intent, formal properties, or implementation timing.
This assertion does not waive, downgrade, or suppress a native warning; every recognized warning is engineering fail under v1alpha1.
This assertion does not claim portability to another parser, language revision, file order, include tree, macro set, or top module.
Selecting 1800-2017 or 1800-2023 requests a Verilator SystemVerilog language edition; it does not certify the implementation or design as conformant to IEEE 1800.
This assertion does not run generated-model simulation or prove that an inferred latch or combinational loop is intentional.

pass.meaning: Verilator completed on the exact bound inputs with complete UTF-8 output capture, exit code zero, and no recognized warning or error.
required_evidence: data.warning_policy is strict, data.environment_policy is closed-verilator-runtime-v1, and data.warning_count, data.error_count, and data.diagnostic_count are all zero.
The ordered source paths, recursively resolved literal include dependencies, stable before/after closure, top module, language revision, tool path, and tool version are retained.
A fresh bounded rtl.lint.log artifact proves complete stdout and stderr capture without truncation.

fail.meaning: Complete trustworthy Verilator output contains at least one recognized warning or error under the strict policy.
required_evidence: data.diagnostic_count is greater than zero and equals data.warning_count plus data.error_count.
Each retained diagnostic preserves its native warning or error severity, stable Verilator code, bounded message, and design-finding classification from the closed v1alpha1 grammar.
The complete fresh lint transcript is retained even when Verilator exits zero because warnings are nonfatal.
Captured inputs and the literal dependency closure remain unchanged across the invocation, and the same closed-verilator-runtime-v1 environment governs the version probe and lint execution.

unknown.meaning: The request, tool availability, execution, diagnostic capture, or native exit classification is insufficient to prove clean or conclusively report a lint finding.
required_evidence: At least one stable diagnostic identifies the invalid input, unavailable or identity-rejected tool, incomplete capture, timeout, unclassified native diagnostic, or unclassified nonzero native exit.
Unknown is never promoted to clean merely because no diagnostic was parsed from incomplete evidence.

configuration_roles.description: One or more existing regular HDL sources whose explicit order is semantically significant.
Optional include roots used to resolve the recursively scanned literal include closure; every resolved dependency is retained by content digest.

semantic_constraints: At least one source is required, source order is preserved exactly, canonical source/include-directory paths and resolved or unresolved dependency identities must contain no ASCII control or DEL character, and no output path may overwrite a source or recursively discovered include dependency.

The top module and every preprocessor definition must satisfy the closed portable token grammar; v1alpha1 accepts no whitespace, quoting, script delimiters, arbitrary native command fragments, or waiver flags.

One closed runtime environment is shared by the version probe and lint execution: PATH contains only the selected executable directory plus the platform default search path, LANG and LC_ALL are C, and ambient loader, interpreter, shell-startup, and VERILATOR_* variables are not inherited.

Verilator is invoked with relative-include resolution and fixed extension mappings for .v, .sv, .vh, and .svh so the recursively hashed literal closure and declared language edition govern native parsing rather than ambient extension defaults.

Every literal include resolved from the sources is scanned transitively and retained as an input dependency; the same closure is rescanned after invocation, while unresolved literal references are disclosed separately because they may occur in inactive preprocessor branches.
The strict policy maps any recognized Verilator warning or error to engineering fail and never relies on the native process exit code alone.

facts.description: The immutable strict policy under which every warning and error is a finding.

The fixed closed environment shared by probe and execution: deterministic PATH and C locale only, plus platform process essentials on Windows; no ambient loader, interpreter, shell-startup, or VERILATOR_* variables.
Total recognized Verilator warning records in complete captured output.
facts.bounds: Zero or greater; zero is required for pass.

facts.description: Total recognized Verilator error records in complete captured output.
facts.bounds: Zero or greater; zero is required for pass.

facts.description: At most 100 bounded native diagnostics; truncation is explicit and never hidden.
Canonical absolute source paths retained in native parse order.
Canonical recursively resolved include dependency paths bound as inputs.
Bounded literal include tokens not resolved by conservative pre-scan; these can occur in inactive preprocessor branches and are disclosed rather than silently treated as active dependencies.
True only when every captured source and resolved include retains the same byte count and SHA-256 after invocation.
True only when a post-run rescan reproduces the ordered sources, resolved literal include closure, and unresolved literal inventory.
True only when the selected Verilator path and filesystem identity stay fixed from before the bounded version probe through execution.
Native records outside the closed v1alpha1 design-finding grammar; any nonzero value forces unknown.

integrity: Create the transcript only from the current invocation.
Require complete valid UTF-8 stdout and stderr capture within the separate one-mebibyte per-stream bounds before pass or fail.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The complete Verilator transcript is authoritative for native warnings and errors; normalized diagnostics are a bounded deterministic projection.

integrity_rules: Hash every source and recursively resolved literal include dependency before native invocation, rehash them afterward, and reproduce the same dependency closure before pass or fail.

Use the same closed-verilator-runtime-v1 environment for the bounded version probe and lint invocation, retaining only deterministic PATH and C locale plus platform process essentials on Windows; do not inherit ambient LD_*, DYLD_*, PERL*, PYTHON*, ENV, BASH_ENV, IFS, CDPATH, SHELLOPTS, or VERILATOR_* keys.
Retain the resolved executable path and reported version with the result.
Stat-bind the selected executable before the bounded version probe, require the inspected path and identity to remain identical after that probe, and recheck the same identity after invocation.
Reject truncated or non-UTF-8 output and unclassified nonzero exits as unknown rather than clean.
provenance_limitations: Literal include scanning may retain files in inactive branches and cannot attest macro-computed includes; it is conservative rather than proof of exact conditional compilation.
Verilator lint semantics, warning inventory, and language support can change across tool versions, so evidence is comparable only with an identical retained tool identity.

Direct verilator_bin discovery is preferred. If an explicit script-wrapper entrypoint is selected, OpenADA binds that selected path and version under the closed environment but does not separately enumerate or hash the wrapper interpreter or its installed companion binary.
No waiver file, project generator, simulator model, external preprocessor, or vendor library outside the bound sources and include roots is represented.

features.request_effect: Select the declared SystemVerilog language revision while preserving file order, include roots, macro definitions, top module, and the strict warning policy.
features.conformance_case: Run a pinned public RTL design to warning-free pass and replay at least one real or injected recognized warning to strict fail while retaining complete native evidence.

diagnostics.condition: A source, include dependency, define, language, top, or output binding is absent, invalid, unsafe, or contains a control-bearing HDL path identity.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the explicit request and bind every required HDL input with control-free canonical paths; do not infer file order, top, macros, or waivers.

diagnostics.condition: The selected runtime cannot resolve Verilator.
diagnostics.next_action: Install or select a pinned Verilator runtime and rerun the exact request.

diagnostics.condition: An executable exists but its bounded version output does not identify supported Verilator.
diagnostics.next_action: Select an identity-validated Verilator build; do not execute a wrong or unversioned binary.

diagnostics.condition: The inspected executable path differs from the selected path, or the selected Verilator filesystem identity changes during the version probe or invocation.
diagnostics.next_action: Freeze the tool installation and PATH selection, then rerun; reject evidence whose version probe is not bound to the executed binary.

diagnostics.condition: The isolated workspace or fresh evidence output cannot be created safely.
diagnostics.next_action: Use a writable fresh evidence directory and rerun without accepting partial output.

diagnostics.condition: The fresh complete transcript cannot be created exclusively or captured as a bounded stable regular file.
diagnostics.next_action: Reject the result and regenerate evidence in a protected fresh directory.

diagnostics.condition: Native output was truncated or was not valid UTF-8.
diagnostics.next_action: Preserve a complete bounded transcript before making a lint decision.

diagnostics.condition: A captured RTL source or include changed during invocation.
diagnostics.next_action: Freeze the source tree and rerun the exact request; do not interpret evidence from moving inputs.

diagnostics.condition: The post-run literal include scan differs from the pre-run source/dependency closure.
diagnostics.next_action: Freeze generated files and include roots, then rerun from a stable source configuration.

diagnostics.condition: The bounded Verilator process or its output-producing process group did not complete before the declared timeout.
diagnostics.next_action: Inspect the retained execution state, choose a justified larger bound or reduce the source scope, and rerun without making a lint conclusion.

diagnostics.condition: OpenADA could not launch, monitor, or complete the selected Verilator process.
diagnostics.next_action: Repair the executable/runtime or isolated workspace and rerun; do not classify the RTL from partial evidence.

diagnostics.condition: The identity-validated Verilator path was no longer executable or available at process launch.
diagnostics.next_action: Freeze the tool installation and rerun preflight plus the exact lint request.

diagnostics.condition: Verilator exited nonzero without a recognized diagnostic in complete output.
diagnostics.next_action: Inspect the native transcript and add a versioned parser mapping; do not classify the RTL as clean.

diagnostics.condition: A native record is outside the closed v1alpha1 design-finding grammar, including option, language, or capability errors.
diagnostics.next_action: Treat the result as unknown; validate the tool capability or add a reviewed versioned mapping before making an RTL claim.

diagnostics.condition: Verilator emitted a warning in the closed v1alpha1 design-finding grammar.
diagnostics.next_action: Resolve or explicitly redesign the RTL condition; v1alpha1 has no waiver mechanism.

diagnostics.condition: Verilator emitted an error in the closed v1alpha1 design-finding grammar.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the cited RTL/top condition and rerun the exact manifest; do not use partial output.

semantic_bindings.implementation: Invoke Verilator lint-only under the closed-verilator-runtime-v1 environment policy with timing, Wall, nonfatal warning capture, relative includes, fixed .v/.sv/.vh/.svh mappings for the declared language edition, and explicit top/include/define/source bindings, then classify complete parsed diagnostics under the strict OpenADA policy.

native_actions: Resolve and hash ordered HDL inputs.
Invoke noninteractive Verilator lint-only without user-supplied native command fragments.
Parse complete native warning and error records and retain the fresh transcript.
limitations: The mapping is strict: warnings and errors both produce engineering fail.
Only SystemVerilog 1800-2017 and 1800-2023 revision selectors are exposed.
Native output must fit the bounded complete capture; otherwise status is unknown.

normative_surfaces: operation, assertion, and strict truth table
ordered source, include, macro, language, and top identity
native diagnostic grammar, sanitized environment, and warning-to-fail policy
normalized diagnostic counts and bounded records
compatibility.change_policy: Any change to warning or environment policy, accepted language or input semantics, native diagnostic classification, evidence completeness, normalized facts, or pass/fail/unknown meaning requires a new profile identifier.
